In 2019, my apprentice A-Jun came to me with 50k. He saved up by working construction for two years and wanted a way out. I told him to take it easy and first learn to 'not lose'. #ZEC 

I taught him three rules: First, split the capital into ten parts, and only use one part per trade. Second, focus on 4-hour breakouts with 5x leverage and a 1.5% stop-loss. Third, when in profit, withdraw half, never touching the principal.

In the first two years, he stumbled a bit. 50k grew to 70k, which isn’t lightning fast, but he never got liquidated.

During the March 2020 crash, while others panicked and sold off, he followed the plan and picked up some spot at the lows, ending that year with over 200k in his account.

In the bull market of 2021, he capitalized on two major waves with ETH and SOL. He moved his stop-loss up to lock in profits, using only gains to add to his position. By the end of the year, his account soared to over 3 million. He stayed grounded and continued trading with small positions.

In the bear market of 2022, he stayed in cash for over half a year, not losing a single dime. In 2023, he resumed dollar-cost averaging, and his account now sits steadily at 8 million.

A-Jun says: 'Bro, I don’t have any special talent; I just repeated your rules for five years.'

From 50k to 8 million wasn’t about chasing hot trades; it was about sticking to a simple strategy. If you can endure, you can make it too.

Can you really turn 1000U into 20 million? I believe it, and here's my story.

Back in 2017, I had just 1000U. Not a rich kid, not a genius, just an average worker. Now my account is over 20 million.

You ask how I did it? Three words: HODL, wait, dollar-cost averaging. $BTC 

During the 2018 bear market, Bitcoin dropped from 20k to 3k. I saved 2000 a month from my salary to DCA into BTC. Back then, no one dared to buy; the chat was filled with “going to zero.” I didn’t care, the more it dropped, the more I bought, and I locked it in a cold wallet. That year, my account was down 60%, and my wife thought I was crazy.

In March 2020, Bitcoin crashed from 9k to 4k. While others were panicking and cutting losses, I followed my plan and swapped all my saved U for BTC and ETH. I managed to bring my cost basis down significantly.

In the 2021 bull market, BTC shot up to 60k, and ETH hit 4k. I sold in three batches: 30% at 40k, another 30% at 50k, and cleared the rest above 60k. On that payday, my account went from that initial 1000U to over 4 million U.

During the bear market, I started DCA again. In 2022, BTC fell to 16k, I bought monthly; in 2023, it rose to 30k, still buying; now it’s skyrocketing to 70k, selling in batches. My account is now safely over 20 million U.
I never opened a futures contract or got liquidated once. It was just buy, wait, and HODL. Am I foolish? Maybe. But those smart folks chasing contracts and meme coins are long gone. $LAB 

Believe it or not, the most profitable method in crypto is often the dullest. If you can wait, the money will wait for you.

Follow Kun Ge, no bragging, no empty promises, just sharing real experiences that help you survive in the market. #LAB #BTC #BILL #ZEC #ETH

In the crypto space, I willingly play the fool.

I used to think I was smart. I learned all kinds of indicators, chased hot news, opened high-leverage contracts, trying to catch every market wave. What was the result? I blew up twice in three years, and my account shrank from 20k U to 3k.
Then I accepted it, and decided to just be a fool. $LAB 

A fool doesn't stare at the charts, only looks at the daily candles. A fool doesn't guess the bottom, only waits for signals. A fool doesn't use leverage, only trades spot. A fool doesn't chase shitcoins, only hoards BTC and ETH.

A fool takes 2,000 bucks from their salary every month for dollar-cost averaging, rain or shine. When it dips, buy more; when it rises, buy less. Once bought, it goes to a cold wallet, password given to the wife, and I don’t check it myself.

In the first year, I was down 30%, and my wife called me foolish. I didn’t budge. In the second year, I broke even; in the third year, a bull market hit, and my account quadrupled. This year, that initial capital has rolled into seven figures.

Those "smart people" are still battling it out in contracts, getting liquidated, then reloading, only to get wrecked again. This fool, however, is still alive and making money.

The crypto world isn't short of smart people; what it lacks are fools who are willing to acknowledge their limits and follow the rules. The more you think you're clever, the faster you lose. If you willingly play the fool, the money will come to you. $BTC 

Follow Kun Ge, an old hand willing to accompany you in playing the fool.

title: Ethereum VS XRP: The Battle of Ecosystem Ideologies
subTitle:
The recent bipartisan push for the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act in the US Senate, and it is obvious that $ ETH and $XRP are no longer competing in the same category. They represent fundamentally opposing views on how blockspace should function.
I observed that $ ETH operates as an open world compute engine where decentralized finance, layer-2 rollups, and liquid staking dictate the supply demand dynamics. The network thrives on organic permissionless economic activity. Conversely, $XRP is optimized entirely for institutional velocity. The core value proposition of the XRP Ledger relies heavily on cross-border liquidity partnerships, acting as a high speed settlement layer rather than an app-hosting platform

In my deep dives into on chain metrics, I find that treating these two assets as direct rivals is a conceptual error. $ ETH demands high computational overhead to maintain its global state machine, while $XRP P prioritizes sub-second finality and cheap settlement metrics at the expense of rich programmability. I do not see one completely replacing the other. Instead, the market is choosing between Ethereum's decentralized application dominance and Ripple's targeted institutional infrastructure. Your choice depends entirely on which network architectural model wins the multi year regulatory and adoption race.
Disclaimer: Educational content only. Not financial advice.

translatedData.content:
Here are some hard truths for new friends; listen up to save yourself a few grand.

When you first enter the crypto scene, stop dreaming about doubling your money. If you don’t even set a stop-loss, why do you think you can make a profit?

First, don’t use your living expenses to trade. If you’re betting money you can’t afford to lose, your mindset is bound to crash. If you lose, you’ll hesitate to cut your losses; if you win, you won’t be able to hold onto your gains. Use spare cash so you can stay cool-headed. $BTC 

Second, don’t jump straight into contracts. With 100x leverage, just a small spike can wipe you out. Even if you didn’t get the direction wrong, your money could vanish. Start with spot trading, learn to read trends, set stop-losses, and then think about leverage.

Third, don’t trust signal groups. The screenshots they flaunt might be fabricated, or just the one profitable trade out of ten. If you dive in, you’re just picking up the bags.

Fourth, don’t go all in. Split your capital into ten parts, and only test the waters with one part at a time. If you lose one part, you still have nine left. If you go all in and it hits zero, there won’t be a next time.

Fifth, if you make a profit, withdraw half right away. The numbers in your account are just digits; only what you withdraw is yours. Stop thinking about compound interest; once you have cash in hand, your mindset stabilizes.

The crypto space isn’t lacking opportunities; what it lacks is you still being alive. First, learn not to lose, then talk about making money. These few words could save you from liquidation, believe it or not.

Build your own trading system, four steps is all you need.

Don’t doubt me, I've seen too many people open trades based purely on "feelings". They feel it’s going up, they jump in; they feel it’s going down, they short it. What happens? They earn without knowing why and lose without understanding why. After three years, their account is halved, and they’re exhausted. $BTC 

Without rules, the market treats you like an ATM.

So how do you build your own trading system? Four steps, just follow along.

Step one, set your direction. Only trade on a timeframe of 4 hours or more. Don’t look at 15-minute charts today and daily charts tomorrow. I stick to the 4-hour chart; if the moving averages are bullish, I only go long, if bearish, I only short. Once the direction is set, no guessing the top or bottom.

Step two, define your pattern. I only recognize one: breakouts from consolidation followed by a retest for confirmation. If that pattern isn’t there, even if it soars, I don’t move. Focus on this one pattern and practice it a hundred times until you can recognize it with your eyes closed.

Step three, determine your position size. Each time, risk only 5%-10% of your total capital. If you lose that, only then do you move to the next trade. Going all in? That’s gambling, not trading.

Step four, set your risk management. A stop loss at 1.5%, if hit, you get out; take profits in batches, withdraw half when you make a profit; if you lose two trades in a row, shut down and take a break.

Write these four steps down, stick them on the side of your screen. Before opening a trade, review them, and only act if all conditions are met.

With rules, the market can’t just harvest your funds. Without rules, you’re just giving it away. $IRYS

Those with big guts have long exited the market, while the cautious ones are still steadily raking in profits.

In the futures market, guts aren't worth much. Being willing to gamble, to hold, or to go all in isn't the real skill. The true value lies in control—controlling your hands to avoid reckless trades, controlling your mind to refrain from adding to profits, and controlling your emotions to prevent revenge trading.

I have a follower who went long on ETH last year with a floating profit of $8000. He was so excited that he wanted to increase his position. I told him to hold back: first, withdraw his initial capital, and let the profits keep rolling in. He followed my advice. Later, when ETH pulled back, he lost half his profit, but he had already gotten his initial capital back, keeping his mindset stable. He said, "If I hadn't controlled myself and added to my position, I might have lost everything during that pullback."

How do you build control? Just three rules: set your position size before opening a trade and don't increase it; withdraw your initial capital when you're up 20%, preventing profits from biting back; if you lose on two consecutive trades, shut down and sleep instead of getting emotional.

translatedData.content:
#ETH  In the crypto game, it's not really about the cash. Even if I hit 48 million, I still feel like it's not enough to spend. 
It's not about showing off; many who have been through it understand this feeling. 
Let me give you a quick background: 
Born in the 90s, graduated from university and moved to Shenzhen, entered the crypto space in 2014. 
Now I'm in Guangzhou, with two houses and two cars, 
spending 100k a month feels like nothing, 
most of my assets are still parked on exchanges. 
But let's be real—trading isn’t romantic anymore. 

Those who have been in the market for the long haul 
have long moved past the phase of "happy when it goes up, sleepless when it drops." 
What’s left are three words: 
repetition, monotony, and battling oneself. 
1. Staying up late isn’t a big deal for us 
For traders, the market is the alarm clock. 
Day and night are the same. 
You’ll notice many so-called “young pros,” 
they're not old, but they look like they’ve aged a decade. 
Your body and emotions are just traded for experience. 
I’d say I’m on the disciplined side. 

2. Stop dreaming about a life of excess 
The reality is more about “going through the motions.” 
Even when I go out to relax, 
there's always a string of tension in my mind. 
It’s not greed; it’s a sense of responsibility. 
The more people trust you, the less you can afford to stop. 
Most of the time isn’t socializing, 
but monitoring the charts, reading sentiment, reviewing, and reflecting. 
Messages on my phone? They never stop piling up. 

3. Pressure never disappears; it just escalates 
At first, I learned how to relieve stress, 
then I practiced how to bear it. 
Answering questions, explaining logic, calibrating positions, 
those who understand know— 
a single point can make a world of difference. 
Finally, here are a few principles I always stick to: 
1️⃣ Don’t rely on feelings; respect the current market sentiment. 
2️⃣ Always have stop-losses; structure and capacity are both essential. 
3️⃣ If the logic is right, take it; if wrong, admit it. 
4️⃣ Trading isn’t about who’s the toughest; it’s about who can go the distance. 
In the end, I found that trading is never about the skills, 
but about managing human nature. 
I hope whoever reads this can slowly outpace 
the one person who can hold you back the most: yourself.

content:
#ETH  币圈最难赢的根本不是钱、哪怕赚到 4800 万，我依然觉得钱不太够花。
不是凡尔赛，是很多经历过的人都会懂的那种感觉。
简单交代下背景：
90 年男，大学毕业去了深圳，2014年进圈。
现在在广州，两套房、两辆车，
每个月拿出 10 万消费基本无感，
大部分资产依然留在交易所。
但说句掏心窝子的——
交易这件事，早就不浪漫了。

真正长期泡在市场里的人，
早就过了“涨一点就开心、跌一点就失眠”的阶段。
剩下的只有三个词：
重复、枯燥、对抗自己。
一、所谓熬夜，在我们这不算什么
对交易员来说，行情就是闹钟。
白天黑夜没有区别。
你会发现很多所谓的“年轻高手”，
年纪不大，却看着比同龄人老一截。
身体和情绪，都是拿来换经验的。
我算比较自律的那一类。

二、别再幻想天天灯红酒绿
真实状态更多是“随便应付”。
就算出去放松，
脑子里也始终绷着一根线。
不是贪，是责任感。
信任你的人越多，你越不敢停。
多数时间不是应酬，
而是盯盘、看情绪、复盘、反思。
手机消息？永远清不完。

三、压力从来不会消失，只会升级
最开始学的是“怎么减压”，
后来练的是“怎么扛压”。
回复问题、解释逻辑、校准位置，
懂的人都知道——
差一个点位，结果天差地别。
最后，说几条我一直死守的原则：
1️⃣ 不靠感觉，尊重当下的市场情绪
2️⃣ 必须有止损，结构和承受力缺一不可
3️⃣ 逻辑对就拿，错了就认
4️⃣ 交易不是比谁猛，是比谁走得远
走到最后才发现，
交易拼的从来不是技术，
而是对人性的管理能力。
希望刷到这篇的你，都能在市场里，
慢慢赢过那个，
最容易拖你后腿的自己。

translatedData.content:
Small capital doesn't fail because it's little,
It fails because of these three things

A buddy of mine had just 800U and wanted to flip it. He jumped right in with a 20x contract, made 200U on the first day, and was over the moon. The next day, the market pulled back, and he didn't cut losses—he held on. By the third day, he got liquidated, left with under 100. He asked me, 'Is it because I had too little money?' I said, 'No, it's not the money; you made three fatal mistakes. $BTC 

First, dying from 'urgency.' Small accounts often want to double up in one shot, opening high leverage, going all in, chasing meme coins. The more urgent you are, the easier it is to lose. Being rushed clouds your judgment; when it's time to cut losses, you think, 'Just hold a bit longer,' and when you should be in cash, you think, 'Let me gamble again.' If you’re not in a hurry, that 800U can grow slowly; when you rush, it's just cannon fodder.

Second, dying from 'greed.' After making some profit, you can't bear to leave, always wanting 'just a bit more.' When profits drop from 200U to 100U, you're still waiting; at 50U, you're even more reluctant to cut. In the end, it all goes to zero. Think about it: doubling 800U only gets you to 1600U; is it worth risking it all?

Third, dying from 'holding on.' You don’t exit when you’re losing, comforting yourself with 'it will come back.' Holding on once might work, but holding on ten times will eventually lead to a liquidation. That little principal can't withstand one big hold. $ETH 

If small capital wants to survive, just three rules: don’t rush, don’t be greedy, don’t hold on. Stick to the rules, take small amounts each time to test the waters; losses won't hurt, and when you make a gain, just run. Survive, and the money will gradually roll in.

Follow Kun Ge, no boasting, no pie-in-the-sky promises, just sharing real experiences that will help you stay alive in the market. #BTC #ETH #ZEC #BILL #UB

content:
小资金不是死在钱少，
是死在这三样东西

一个兄弟，手里就800U，想翻身。他上来就开了20倍合约，第一天赚了200U，兴奋得不行。第二天行情回调，他不止损，扛。第三天爆仓，剩不到100。他问我：“是不是钱太少了？”我说不是钱少，是你犯了三个致命毛病。$BTC 

第一，死在“急”。 小资金总想一把翻倍，开高倍、梭哈、追土狗。你越急，越容易亏。因为急会让你失去判断力，该止损的时候想“再扛一下”，该空仓的时候想“再搏一把”。你不急，这800U能慢慢磨；你一急，它就是炮灰。

第二，死在“贪”。 赚了点舍不得走，总想“再拿拿”。利润从200U变100U，还在等；变成50U，更舍不得割。最后归零。你不想想，800U翻倍也就1600U，值得你拿命去赌吗？

第三，死在“扛”。 亏了不走，安慰自己“会回来的”。扛一次可能回来，扛十次总有一次爆。你那点本金，经不起一次扛。$ETH 

小资金想活，就三句话：别急、别贪、别扛。把规矩焊死，每次只拿一点点钱试，亏了不疼，赚了就跑。你活下来了，钱自然会慢慢滚。

关注坤哥，不吹牛、不画饼，只分享能让你在市场里活下去的真经验。#BTC #ETH #ZEC #BILL #UB

translatedData.content:
Newbies trading futures, first learn to survive, then think about making money#新手必看 

Don't jump in and immediately fixate on "doubling" your money. If you don't even understand how to avoid liquidation, why do you think you can profit?

I've seen the wildest newbies: entering with 1000U, going all in with 20x leverage, and not setting a stop-loss. Three days later, they're liquidated and come asking, "Is the market bad?" I tell them it's not the market; they didn't even leave themselves a safety net.

For newbies trading futures, there are three survival rules; if you can't remember them, don't even touch it.$ETH 

First, split your funds into small portions. Take 1000U and break it into 10 parts, only risking 100U per trade. If you lose that 100U, stop—no moving to the next chunk. If you go all in and get wiped out, there won’t be a next time.

Second, don’t leverage over 5x. Within 5x, you have some breathing room. Above 20x, even a small fluctuation can wipe you out.

Third, set your stop-loss in stone. The first thing after opening a position is to set a stop-loss at 1.5%; if it hits, cut it. No fantasies, don’t wait for a rebound.

Practice with 100U, aiming for three months without liquidation. Once you master that, then we can talk about making money. If you can’t manage that, giving you 10,000U would just be a waste.

Newbie lesson one: learn how not to die first. Surviving is the only way to flip your position.$BTC 

Follow Kun Ge, no boasting, no empty promises, just sharing real experiences that can help you stay in the market.#BTC #ETH #ZEC #BILL

content:
小白玩合约，先学保命，再想赚钱#新手必看 

别一上来就盯着“翻倍”。你连怎么不爆都没搞懂，凭什么觉得自己能赚？

我见过最离谱的新手：1000U进场，开20倍梭哈，不挂止损。三天爆仓，跑来问“是不是行情不好”。我说不是行情不好，是你连命都没给自己留。

小白玩合约，就三条保命规矩，记不住别碰。$ETH 

第一，钱拆小份。 1000U拆成10份，每次只拿100U开单。这100U亏完就停，绝不用下一份。你全仓进去，一次归零就没下次了。

第二，杠杆别过5倍。 5倍以内，你还有腾挪的余地。20倍以上，一个毛刺就能送你走。

第三，止损焊死。 开单第一件事挂止损，1.5%，到了闭眼砍。不幻想，不等反弹。

你用100U去练，目标是连续三个月不爆仓。练出来了，再谈赚钱。练不出来，给你一万U也是送。

新手第一课：先学怎么不死。活着，才有资格翻盘。$BTC 

关注坤哥，不吹牛、不画饼，只分享能让你在市场里活下去的真经验。#BTC #ETH #ZEC #BILL
